question: A 'for loop' in programming
options: A special method that initialises an object when it is created
Repeats a block of code a fixed number of times
A class can acquire properties and methods from a parent class
A blueprint or template for creating objects
answer: Repeats a block of code a fixed number of times

question: A 'while loop' continues
options: As long as a specified condition remains true
A special method that initialises an object when it is created
Objects that contain data (attributes) and functions (methods)
Hiding complex implementation details and showing only essential features
answer: As long as a specified condition remains true

question: The concept of 'recursion' in programming
options: Repeats a block of code a fixed number of times
A class can acquire properties and methods from a parent class
A function that calls itself to solve smaller instances of a problem
A contract specifying methods a class must implement without providing implementation
answer: A function that calls itself to solve smaller instances of a problem

question: 'Object-oriented programming' is based on
options: Bundling data and methods together and restricting direct access
Repeats a block of code a fixed number of times
Objects that contain data (attributes) and functions (methods)
Hiding complex implementation details and showing only essential features
answer: Objects that contain data (attributes) and functions (methods)

question: The concept of 'inheritance' in OOP
options: Belongs to the class rather than any specific instance
Objects that contain data (attributes) and functions (methods)
Objects of different classes can be treated as objects of a common parent class
A class can acquire properties and methods from a parent class
answer: A class can acquire properties and methods from a parent class

question: 'Encapsulation' in OOP means
options: Objects of different classes can be treated as objects of a common parent class
Bundling data and methods together and restricting direct access
Hiding complex implementation details and showing only essential features
A function that calls itself to solve smaller instances of a problem
answer: Bundling data and methods together and restricting direct access

question: The concept of 'polymorphism' in OOP
options: A subclass provides its own implementation of a method inherited from a parent class
A special method that initialises an object when it is created
Objects of different classes can be treated as objects of a common parent class
Bundling data and methods together and restricting direct access
answer: Objects of different classes can be treated as objects of a common parent class

question: 'Abstraction' in OOP
options: Hiding complex implementation details and showing only essential features
Creating a specific object from a class
Bundling data and methods together and restricting direct access
A subclass provides its own implementation of a method inherited from a parent class
answer: Hiding complex implementation details and showing only essential features

question: A 'class' in OOP is
options: A contract specifying methods a class must implement without providing implementation
Creating a specific object from a class
A blueprint or template for creating objects
Multiple methods with the same name but different parameters
answer: A blueprint or template for creating objects

question: The concept of 'instantiation'
options: Bundling data and methods together and restricting direct access
Creating a specific object from a class
As long as a specified condition remains true
A class can acquire properties and methods from a parent class
answer: Creating a specific object from a class

question: A 'constructor' in OOP
options: Creating a specific object from a class
A special method that initialises an object when it is created
A subclass provides its own implementation of a method inherited from a parent class
Belongs to the class rather than any specific instance
answer: A special method that initialises an object when it is created

question: 'Method overloading' allows
options: A function that calls itself to solve smaller instances of a problem
Objects that contain data (attributes) and functions (methods)
Repeats a block of code a fixed number of times
Multiple methods with the same name but different parameters
answer: Multiple methods with the same name but different parameters

question: The concept of 'method overriding'
options: Multiple methods with the same name but different parameters
As long as a specified condition remains true
A contract specifying methods a class must implement without providing implementation
A subclass provides its own implementation of a method inherited from a parent class
answer: A subclass provides its own implementation of a method inherited from a parent class

question: A 'static method' in OOP
options: Multiple methods with the same name but different parameters
Hiding complex implementation details and showing only essential features
Belongs to the class rather than any specific instance
Objects of different classes can be treated as objects of a common parent class
answer: Belongs to the class rather than any specific instance

question: The concept of 'interface' in OOP
options: Objects that contain data (attributes) and functions (methods)
Belongs to the class rather than any specific instance
A contract specifying methods a class must implement without providing implementation
A function that calls itself to solve smaller instances of a problem
answer: A contract specifying methods a class must implement without providing implementation
